name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Montana Capital offers title loans from $100–$50,000 with same-day funding, allowing borrowers to keep their vehicle while using the title as collateral.
description_long:
Montana Capital has been operating as a title loan agency since 2007, helping over 375,000 customers access emergency cash. The company specializes in secured title loans, where borrowers pledge their vehicle's title to access funds while retaining driving privileges. Their primary target market includes individuals with poor or no credit history who need rapid access to cash and may be turned down by traditional lenders.

Montana Capital's core offering is title loans ranging from $100 to $50,000, with pre-approval available in under 5 minutes and same-day or next-day funding options. The application process is entirely online or in-person, with no credit check required. Borrowers keep their vehicle keys and continue driving while repaying the loan. Once final payment is made, the title is returned to the borrower. The company operates 24/7 online access and maintains multiple physical locations.

The company distinguishes itself through fast turnaround (same-day funding claimed), no credit impact during pre-approval, explicit acceptance of applicants with bad credit and bankruptcies, and customer-reported competitive rates compared to other title loan providers. Google reviews average 4.5 stars across 59 reviews, with customers highlighting friendly staff, quick processing, and clear explanations of terms. One reviewer noted the loan disburses as a Bank of America check rather than cash.

The critical caveat is that title loans are high-cost secured debt. While the website does not disclose APR or specific loan terms, title loans are regulated consumer products typically carrying 25–300% APR depending on jurisdiction and loan terms. Borrowers risk vehicle repossession if they default, despite retaining driving privileges during repayment. The marketing emphasizes speed and accessibility for underbanked consumers but does not transparently display cost comparisons or debt counseling alternatives.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available with approval in under 5 minutes
No credit check required; explicitly accepts customers with bad credit and bankruptcies
Borrowers keep their car and keys while repaying the loan
Loan amounts up to $50,000 available, significantly higher than typical payday loans
24/7 online application and access to services
Google rating of 4.5 stars with customers reporting faster rates than competitors
Multiple physical locations plus online options for convenience
cons: Title loans typically carry very high APR (25–300% depending on jurisdiction), though specific rates are not disclosed on the website
Vehicle is held as collateral and subject to repossession if loan defaults
Funds disburse as Bank of America check rather than immediate cash, per customer review
Website lacks transparent disclosure of total cost of borrowing, payment terms, or APR ranges
Target market (low-credit borrowers needing emergency cash) is vulnerable to debt spirals with high-cost secured loans
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit history who need rapid emergency cash and have no other borrowing options
Borrowers who can repay within a short timeframe and understand the high cost structure
Individuals who need $2,000–$50,000 and have significant vehicle equity to pledge as collateral
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best for vehicle owners with poor credit who need emergency cash quickly and have significant equity in their car. The main caveat is that title loans are high-cost secured debt with rates typically ranging from 25–300% APR; borrowers risk vehicle repossession and should exhaust lower-cost alternatives (credit unions, payment plans, employer advances) before pursuing a title loan.
services: Online title loan applications with 24/7 access
In-person title loan applications at physical locations
Free pre-approval in under 5 minutes with no credit score impact
Same-day or next-day loan funding options
Vehicle equity assessment and loan amount qualification
Direct deposit or check disbursement of loan funds
Simple loan payoff and title return process
